violations.text: Raw animal foods (eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (drinks) in the reach-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2024
No person in charge was present as needed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 06/18/2024

Milk, eggs, sour cream and cream were observed held at 55-61 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC informed that no TCS foods to be stored in walk-in cooler until it reaches a temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2024
Drinks were observed stored on the floor in the retail area. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2024

At the time of inspection, ambient of walk-in cooler was at 55 degrees Fahrenheit. No TCS foods are to be stored in unit until serviced and at consistent temperatures at or below 41 degrees Fahrenheit. PIC to send proof of repairs to inspector before storing TCS foods. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2024

Milk, cheese, lunchables, eggs and deli meats were observed held at 49-51 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. See disposal. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2024
Rodenticide was observed under retail shelving. PIC informed to remove from premises. New Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2024
Drinks were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2024

At the time of inspection, ambient of walk-in cooler was at 47 degrees Fahrenheit. No TCS foods are to be stored in unit until serviced and at consistent temperatures at/below 41 degrees Fahrenheit. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2024
At the time of inspection, walk-in cooler fan has an accumulation of frost buildup. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2024

The floors in the retail area has an accumulation of rodent droppings and debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2024

Employees' personal belongings were observed stored in the walk-in cooler. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. New Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2024
Grain beetles observed in bags of various dried foods (rice and flours). Foods were voluntarily disposed by the PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/22/2023

Raw animal foods (such as eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as milk ) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/22/2023

Dried foods packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. Foods removed from retail sale during time of inspection. Foods cannot be sold until properly labelled. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/22/2023

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings and adulterated foods. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 08/22/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the 1 door freezers have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 08/29/2023

The condenser in the walk-in cooler has an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 08/29/2023
